forecast-period.text: A low pressure trough moving over the far west will slip southeastwards on Monday. A series of low pressure systems will pass over waters to south during the week with associated cold fronts crossing over SA.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Isolated showers possible in all districts, though most likely about the northeast. Chance of a thunderstorm about the pastoral and West Coast districts, though most about the far northeast. Maximum temperatures near or slightly below average. Northerly winds, fresh at times, turning westerly in the far west.

Morning fog patches. Isolated showers possible about central and eastern districts, and developing about the far west later. Possible thunderstorms about the far north east and western coasts. Maximum temperatures near average though tending to above average above the north. North to northwesterly winds fresh at times.

Showers and isolated thunderstorms about the west extending eastwards during the day. Showers contracting to the Southern Agricultural Area during the evening. Maximum temperatures below average about the west and tending to above average about the northeast. Fresh and gusty north to northwesterly winds, tending westerly in the west.

text.p: Showers about the Agricultural Area possibly extending to south of the pastoral districts. Possible thunderstorms about southern coasts. Maximum temperatures below average. Fresh and gusty northwesterly winds, ahead of westerly change extending throughout.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Thursday: broadly 5-15 mm with falls of 15-30 mm possible about central and eastern districts. Local falls of 30-50 mm about Kangaroo Island. Mount Lofty Ranges and parts of the southeast.
